title: Selaginella inaequalifolia
taxonomy: Selaginella inaequalifolia is a species of spikemoss in the family Selaginellaceae. It belongs to the genus Selaginella and the order Selaginellales. Within the plant kingdom, it falls under the division Pteridophyta and the kingdom Plantae. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other spikemosses. The precise classification is supported by current botanical and phylogenetic research.

intro: Selaginella inaequalifolia is a type of spore-producing plant, specifically a species of resurrection fern, known for its unique ability to revive itself after drying out. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella inaequalifolia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Selaginella inaequalifolia is often prepared as an infusion or a decoction, and it is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that are consumed to promote overall well-being and alleviate specific health complaints.
